other_observers: I observed the bird with Maggie Boswell, Peter Gent, and John Vanderpoel.; Bird first found, photographed, and reported by Thomas Heinrich.
description: Medium-sized brownish larid. Capped appearance, distinct central tail feather projection, and undertail covert barring typical of young jaeger. About the size of a Ring-billed Gull. Small (for a jaeger) bill, distal half black, proximal half gray. Dark eye completely in brown cap that runs from upper nape to gape. Cheek to lower nape creamy pale brown. Upperparts grayish brown, back feathers tipped with pale brown giving barred appearance. Outer two primary shafts quite white, the rest more uniform dark gray with paler inner webs. Upper- and undertail coverts strongly barred. Spread tail diamond-shaped, with extended inner two recs. Throat mostly same creamy pale brown as nape and cheek, transitioning to darker bib on upper breast. Bib had vertically striped appearance, likely due to oiling or other pollutant rather than actual vertical striping of the feathers. Wing projection long, about even but slightly overhanging tail tip.Behaviors: Mostly swimming around, picking food from surface (quite similar behavior to a feeding Sabine's Gull.) Occasionally seen on short flights, only to land again and resume feeding. No harassment of gulls seen while I was there.Call: not heardPlumage: juvenile
